item.title: Why Cognition bought Poke: AI personality is becoming a competitive advantage
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: AI coding startup Cognition has acquired Poke, the AI assistant you text like a friend, in a deal valuing the startup in the low nine figures. The acquisition brings Poke’s conversational style and interaction model to Cognition’s coding agent Devin, reflecting a growing belief that how AI assistants interact with users is as important as the models powering them.

item.title: US accuses American of allegedly wiping his phone using a ‘duress’ password during border search
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: A U.S. citizen has asked a court to throw out the government's claim that he gave over a passcode to border authorities that wiped his phone's data, opening up fresh questions about a person's constitutional rights at the U.S. border.

item.title: Anduril reportedly in talks to raise funding at $100B valuation, more than 3x last year’s mark
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Anduril is said to be raising a new round of funding that may push its valuation up to about $100 billion, per Reuters.

item.title: Build in public, fail in public: what it’s like to be a founder under 20 right now
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: AI tools have democratized the opportunity to build, shortening the timelines of success and enabling more young people to start successful companies without stepping foot inside a Big Tech company.

item.title: Anthropic launches Opus 5
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Opus 5 will be both cheaper and less restrictive than Fable, likely making it preferable in most use cases.

item.title: India’s move against Jack Dorsey’s Bitchat sparks legal debate
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The offline messaging app surged in popularity in India amid protests in New Delhi.

item.title: OpenAI’s own model went rogue before Kimi had Wall Street sweating
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Chinese AI lab Moonshot’s open model Kimi went viral this week for reasons that had less to do with the model itself and more to do with how the U.S. AI industry reacted to it. Meanwhile, an unreleased OpenAI model wandered outside its test environment and ended up connected to a real security breach at Hugging Face — a reminder […]

item.title: Sam Altman’s biometric startup World raises $52.5M via crypto sale
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Sam Altman's side project — which seeks to scan the world's eyeballs and turn them into unique digital identifiers — has raised some fresh cash through a crypto sale.

item.title: As US weighs response to Chinese AI, industry urges against broad open-weight restrictions
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: AI companies, including Nvidia and Mistral, urge policymakers to avoid broad restrictions on open-weight AI models as Washington debates responses to Chinese AI and alleged model distillation.

item.title: Rivian sues the US government for ‘full refund’ of Trump tariffs
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The automaker joins a long line of companies seeking such refunds. In April, Rivian CFO Claire McDonough said she expected the company stood to reap a refund in the "tens of millions of dollars."

item.title: Bluesky’s AI assistant Attie expands into an open social research tool
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Users can now ask Attie questions about news, trends, and conversations on Bluesky and other apps on the AT Protocol.

item.title: Midjourney acquired the astrology app Co-Star
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: The AI lab Midjourney continues to expand its purview beyond image and video generation.

item.title: OpenAI’s new voice mode makes it to the ChatGPT desktop app
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: ChatGPT Voice on desktop can work with both ChatGPT Work and Codex to complete tasks and control agents.

item.title: Facebook launches a dedicated Marketplace app for sellers, adds a free verification system
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Seller is a dedicated Marketplace app for people who list and sell items frequently.

item.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

item.title: Meet the judges who will crown Australia’s next breakout startup
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: TechCrunch Startup Battlefield is coming to Australia — and we're partnering with Stripe to find the country's most exciting early-stage startups.

item.title: Mobileye CEO Amnon Shashua to step aside as company pushes into robotaxis, robotics
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Shashua has been invited to take the chairman of the board seat.

item.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

item.title: Patreon lays off 20% of its workforce
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: In a memo to staff that was posted online, Conte said the company's core business is strong, but the platform has to respond to market changes and adjust its cost structure to remain stable.

item.title: Insurance startup Corgi reportedly raised more money at $4B — its third round in 8 weeks
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: In the AI-funding frenzy, many startups are raising back-to-back rounds at ever-increasing valuations — but even by those standards, Corgi stands out.
